title: One African nation starts to 'push back' on abusive foreign investors [Zambia] - Business and Human Rights Centre
impact_evidence: Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Worker health and safety risks -> "has started revoking licenses of local and foreign investors that abuse incentives – ranging from ignoring safety standards" | "[T]he Zambian government has taken away the licenses of the Chinese-owned Collum Coal Mine on grounds of poor safety and health standards among its employees." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Employment creation and job security -> "breaking promises to hire locals" | "In 2013, Konkola Copper Mines, owned by...Vedanta Resources...announced...that it was preparing to fire...workers..." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Corporate transparency and reporting -> "perpetuating a lack of transparency" || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Mining permitting and licensing practices -> "has started revoking licenses of local and foreign investors that abuse incentives" | "[T]he Zambian government has taken away the licenses of the Chinese-owned Collum Coal Mine on grounds of poor safety and health standards among its employees." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> "Mr. Kumar's work permit was subsequently revoked [by the government], and he was barred from entering Zambia"
mine_name: Collum Coal Mine; Konkola Copper Mines

title: Nationalizing Zambia's copper mines
impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> ""The people who are going to retire, where is the money going to come from?" Tembo posed. "If the government is taking over, they have to do it in the quickest way by paying off the retirees," Tembo told DW." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Employment creation and job security -> "More than 73,000 people are employed in Zambia's extractive industry, representing 2.4% of the workforce in the southern African nation of nearly 18 million people." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Business opportunities (local supply chains, services) -> "If big companies slow down on big investments because of Mopani Copper Mines' fate, mining consultant Ron Smit said that developments in the sector are likely to stall." || Social | Equity, Rights & Vulnerable Groups | Wealth distribution and inequality -> ""The mines need to belong to the government and not foreigners. The opposite means the country's minerals only benefit foreigners," Emmanuel Chisala, a Lusaka resident, told DW." || Governance | Reputation & Social License | Trust between mining companies, government, and communities -> ""These actions by government that look like they are unilateral, are never, never good in any country," Smit told DW. "You would really hope for a sector where government and the industry are in better conversations with each other so that these issues can be avoided."" || Governance | Reputation & Social License | Mining image and public perception -> ""It is sending another signal, whether real or perceived, that Zambia is a difficult environment in which to do business." Chibamba Kanyama, a Zambian economist, told DW."

impact_level3: Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation || Employment creation and job security || Business opportunities (local supply chains, services) || Wealth distribution and inequality || Trust between mining companies, government, and communities || Mining image and public perception

title: BeMetals Corp.: BeMetals to Accelerate Copper Exploration at Pangeni Project in Zambia with JOGMEC Exploration Investment
impact_evidence: Governance | Stakeholder Engagement & Rights | Stakeholder inclusion in decision-making -> "Upon completion of the above investment, JOGMEC will have earned 27.8% interest of BeMetals' option of up to 72% interest in the Property, which interest is to be maintained through ongoing 27.8% pro-rata funding of the Project's future exploration expenditures." | "BeMetals retains overall management control through the Project's technical committee."
